title: Charles V becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Charles V was Holy Roman Emperor and Archduke of Austria from 1519 to 1556, King of Spain from 1516 to 1556, King of Sicily and Naples from 1516 to 1554, and also Lord of the Netherlands and titular Duke of Burgundy from 1506 to 1555.

title: Charles II of Spain becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Charles II was the king of Spain from 1665 to 1700.

title: Philip II of Spain becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Philip II, sometimes known in Spain as Philip the Prudent, was King of Spain from 1556, King of Portugal from 1580, and King of Naples and Sicily from 1554 until his death in 1598.

title: Philip V of Spain becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Philip V was king of Spain from 1 November 1700 to 14 January 1724 and again from 6 September 1724 to his death in 1746.

title: Philip IV of Spain becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Philip IV, also called the Planet King, was King of Spain from 1621 to his death and King of Portugal from 1621 to 1640.

title: Ferdinand II of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Ferdinand II was King of Aragon from 1479 until his death in 1516.

title: Philip III of Spain becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Philip III was King of Spain and Portugal during the period known as the Iberian Union, reigning from 1598 until his death in 1621.

title: Charles VI, Holy Roman Emperor becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Charles VI was Holy Roman Emperor and ruler of the Austrian Habsburg monarchy from 1711 until his death, succeeding his elder brother, Joseph I.

title: James I of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: James I the Conqueror was King of Aragon, Count of Barcelona, and Lord of Montpellier from 1213 to 1276; King of Majorca from 1231 to 1276; and King of Valencia from 1238 to 1276.

title: Alfonso V of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Alfonso the Magnanimous was King of Aragon and King of Sicily and the ruler of the Crown of Aragon from 1416 and King of Naples from 1442 until his death.

title: Ferdinand I of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Ferdinand I named Ferdinand of Antequera and also the Just was king of Aragon, Valencia, Majorca, Sardinia and (nominally) Corsica and king of Sicily, duke (nominal) of Athens and Neopatria, and count of Barcelona, Roussillon and Cerdanya (1412–1416).

title: John II of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: John II, called the Great or the Faithless, was King of Aragon from 1458 until his death in 1479.

title: James II of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: James II, called the Just, was the King of Aragon and Valencia and Count of Barcelona from 1291 to 1327.

title: Alfonso II of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Alfonso II, called the Chaste or the Troubadour, was the King of Aragon and, as Alfons I, the Count of Barcelona from 1164 until his death.

title: Peter IV of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Peter IV, called the Ceremonious, was from 1336 until his death the king of Aragon, Sardinia-Corsica, and Valencia, and count of Barcelona.

title: Martin of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Martin the Humane, also called the Elder and the Ecclesiastic, was King of Aragon, Valencia, Sardinia and Corsica and Count of Barcelona from 1396 and King of Sicily from 1409.

title: Peter II of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Peter II the Catholic was the King of Aragon and Count of Barcelona from 1196 to 1213.

title: John I of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: John I, called by posterity the Hunter or the Lover of Elegance, or the Abandoned in his lifetime, was the King of Aragon from 1387 until his death.

title: Alfonso IV of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Alfonso IV, called the Kind, was King of Aragon and Count of Barcelona from 1327 to his death.

title: Alfonso III of Aragon becomes king of the Crown of Aragon
fact: Alfonso III, called the Liberal and the Free, was king of Aragon and Valencia, and count of Barcelona from 1285 until his death.
